paper.title: Effect of Newspapers on Voter Turnout
paper.description: This paper estimates the effect of newspaper entry on political participation in US elections from 1868 to 1928, using county-level panel data on daily newspapers and presidential voter turnout.

methodology.identification_strategy: Non-normalized event-study DID using did_multiplegt_dyn (de Chaisemartin & D'Haultfoeuille, 2024). Standard errors clustered at county level.
methodology.code: did_multiplegt_dyn lnVoters lnPapers year county, graph_off placebo(4) effects(4) cluster(county)

qa.reviewer_comments: Are the pre-treatment trends statistically significant? The placebos look clean.
qa.author_response: No, none of the placebo estimates are significant at the 5% level, supporting the parallel trends assumption.
